briefs.title: Council to Consider Letter Urging Federal Action on Chlorine Rail Transport Safety Risks
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council voted unanimously to send a letter to the federal Transport Minister raising serious safety concerns about liquid chlorine being transported by rail through densely populated areas of the Lower Mainland. The letter responds to a proposed bylaw amendment in the District of North Vancouver that could allow a chlorine plant to keep operating past 2030.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near a Burnaby rail corridor, monitor this issue — a chlorine release could trigger emergency closures.

key_dates.label: District of North Vancouver chlorine bylaw amendment — third reading scheduled
